context: Tests whether the agent implements the correct lifecycle stage taxonomy, classification logic with proper thresholds, a batch-safe nightly job, and a hysteresis mechanism to prevent stage thrashing.

checklist.name: 8 exact stage names
checklist.description: The LifecycleStage type includes all of: 'anonymous', 'subscriber', 'first-time-buyer', 'active', 'loyal', 'at-risk', 'lapsed', 'advocate' (no extra stages, no renamed stages)

checklist.name: CustomerLifecycle interface shape
checklist.description: A CustomerLifecycle interface (or equivalent) exists with at minimum: customerId (string), stage, enteredStageAt (Date), previousStage (optional), and metadata fields

checklist.name: Anonymous check uses emailVerified
checklist.description: The stage assignment checks customer.emailVerified (or equivalent) to return 'anonymous' — not absence of email address alone

checklist.name: First-time-buyer 60-day threshold
checklist.description: The 'first-time-buyer' stage requires exactly 1 order AND the last order was placed within 60 days (daysSince <= 60)

checklist.name: Loyal thresholds correct
checklist.description: The 'loyal' stage is assigned when LTV >= 500 OR order count >= 4 (both conditions handled as OR, not AND)

checklist.name: At-risk uses purchase frequency
checklist.description: The 'at-risk' classification computes average purchase frequency and triggers when daysSince > avgFreq AND daysSince < avgFreq * 2

checklist.name: Lapsed uses 2x frequency
checklist.description: The 'lapsed' classification triggers when daysSince > avgFreq * 2 (or no prior order exists)

checklist.name: Nightly batch job present
checklist.description: An updateLifecycleStages (or equivalent) function exists that iterates all email-verified customers and re-evaluates their stage

checklist.name: Batch chunking / pagination
checklist.description: The nightly batch job processes customers in chunks (e.g. batches of 1000) using cursor pagination rather than loading all records at once

checklist.name: Hysteresis mechanism
checklist.description: The implementation includes a mechanism to require a stage to be stable across 2 consecutive evaluations before triggering a workflow transition (prevents daily stage flipping)

checklist.name: Stage count <= 8
checklist.description: The implementation does NOT define more than 8 lifecycle stages (no micro-stages like 'new-subscriber', 'semi-loyal', etc.)
